commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes guarded trusted-tool-policy registry, diagnostic, evaluation, and decision reads, then adds focused contract and before-tool-call coverage for fail-closed behavior.

PR surface: Source +352, Tests +358. Total +710 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reads trusted-policy registry entries and evaluates policy results without guards. I did not run tests because this review was read-only, but the PR adds focused failing-shape coverage for the path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review should decide whether the compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior is the intended trusted-policy contract; no narrow automation repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- fails closed when trusted tool policy registration, registry iteration, policy evaluation, decision fields, or policy params are unreadable or malformed
- preserves compatible no-op policy results such as `undefined`, optional fields, and empty `{}` decisions
- keeps before-tool-call diagnostics on guarded policy snapshots instead of raw registry reads
- intentionally prunes deeper approval-option enum policing so this stays focused on crash/fail-open prevention
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- replayed the pruned trusted-policy slice onto current `origin/main` 5c5711f061cd after old CI failures traced to stale-base extension type drift outside this PR
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 routed shards, 113 tests passed on head d9ed1adfa467
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check` plus touched-file scrub for reported fixture names and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed trusted policy state no longer throws open or crashes diagnostics; unreadable or malformed policy inputs fail closed while valid no-op policy decisions stay compatible.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree on current `origin/main` 5c5711f061cd with focused host-hook contract and before-tool-call tests on head d9ed1adfa467.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: replayed the pruned trusted-policy slice onto current main, ran focused host-hook and before-tool-call tests, formatter check, targeted lint check, diff check, touched-file scrub, and autoreview.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable policy registries, unreadable policy entries, throwing policy evaluation, unreadable decisions, malformed concrete decisions, unreadable params, and guarded diagnostic policy state fail closed without breaking healthy policy chains.

Observed result after fix: a malformed trusted policy cannot silently allow a tool call or crash diagnostic emission before the assistant path can continue handling the failure.

What was not tested: no full live plugin install with a hostile trusted-policy plugin and no separate local Testbox run beyond GitHub PR checks. GitHub PR checks on `d9ed1adfa467` were successful after the PR-body refresh. Behavior is covered by host-hook contract and before-tool-call tests.
